records.stage_evidence: No procedural devices or contrast media visible; diagnostic imaging
records.summary: A posteroanterior chest X-ray showing bilateral lung fields with diffuse opacities, particularly prominent in the lower zones, suggesting possible pulmonary consolidation or infiltrates. The cardiac silhouette is visible, and bony structures including ribs and clavicles are intact.
key_observations: Diffuse bilateral pulmonary opacities, more pronounced in lower lung zones
Image includes patient identifier and date: 26-Mar-2026

limitations: Vertical artifacts or streaks across the image
Partial obscuration of lung fields by artifacts

records.stage_evidence: Radiology report describing findings from a chest X-ray

ocr.verbatim:
Reg. No.
Patient Name : 
Patient UHID : 177601
Age/Sex :72Y/M
Date :26/03/2026
X Rav CHEST PA VIEW
REPORT
Trachea is central.
Cardiac size appears within normal limits.
Bilateral lung fields show patchy air-space opacities, more prominent in the lower zones, especially on the left side.
Costophrenic angles appear clear.
No obvious pneumothorax seen.
Visualized bony thorax appears intact.
Impression: B/L Pneumonitis.
Advised -clinical correlation.
Radiologist:

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for a chest X-ray (PA view) performed on a 72-year-old male patient on March 26, 2026. The report notes patchy air-space opacities in the bilateral lung fields, more prominent in the lower zones, especially the left side, and concludes with an impression of bilateral pneumonitis, advising clinical correlation.
key_observations: Findings: Patchy air-space opacities in bilateral lung fields, more prominent in lower zones (especially left side)
Normal cardiac size, clear costophrenic angles, no pneumothorax, intact bony thorax
